name: Fire-and-forget create_task in shutdown/signal handler path
description: asyncio.create_task() called in a signal handler or shutdown path with no stored handle. If the coroutine raises before completing, the exception is silently dropped after GC collects the task. In a shutdown path this can cause resources to not be released — connections left open, buffers unflushed, locks not released.
context: asyncio signal handler or shutdown path

bad_example.code:
def _signal_handler(signum, frame):
    logger.info("Received SIGTERM, stopping...")
    asyncio.create_task(self.stop_mqtt())  # fire and forget

async def stop_mqtt(self):
    await self._client.disconnect()
    await self._subscriber_task.cancel()
    self._connected = False
bad_example.why_bad: asyncio.create_task() called from a synchronous signal handler. The task handle is never stored. If stop_mqtt() raises (e.g. disconnect timeout), the exception is swallowed and _connected remains True, causing the next health check to report falsely healthy state. Also: calling create_task() from a sync signal handler requires the event loop to already be running — if it is not, this silently fails to schedule the coroutine.

good_example.code:
def _signal_handler(signum, frame):
    logger.info("Received SIGTERM, scheduling stop...")
    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    task = loop.create_task(self.stop_mqtt())
    task.add_done_callback(
        lambda t: logger.error("stop_mqtt failed: %s", t.exception())
        if t.exception() else None
    )
    self._shutdown_tasks.append(task)
good_example.why_better: Handle stored, done callback logs failures, task tracked for clean shutdown join.

detection.note: Also grep for create_task inside functions named _signal_handler, on_signal, handle_sigterm

source: deep_think_mcp scan 2026-06-03; engine/mqtt_tasks.py:208 (bare fire-and-forget in shutdown path)
